pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 18 defines HDRI_FILE path; consistent with task.json metadata
Downloaded from https://dl.polyhaven.org/file/ph-assets/HDRIs/exr/1k/studio_small_09_1k.exr and verified as valid OpenEXR image (1024x512, 1.3MB, OpenEXR version 2, piz compression)
setup_task.sh line 17; HDRI is from Poly Haven asset library (https://polyhaven.com/a/studio_small_09), a real public resource
export_result.sh line 8 (OUTPUT_BLEND) and task.json description line 7
export_result.sh line 9 (OUTPUT_RENDER) and task.json description line 6
task.json description line 4: 'Set the Mapping Z-Rotation to 125 degrees to position the key light'
Mathematical conversion: 125 degrees × π/180 = 2.181661564... ≈ 2.18166 (truncated to 5 decimal places)
verifier.py line 60 uses tolerance of 0.2 radians (11.46 degrees), not 0.1 radians (5.73 degrees) as claimed in metadata
task.json description line 5: 'set Saturation to 0.0 to make the lighting purely black and white'
verifier.py line 71 checks 'if sat <= 0.05:' to award points for saturation validation

privileged_info_summary: The HDRI Studio Noir task uses a real HDRI file (studio_small_09_1k.exr from Poly Haven, 1024x512 pixels, OpenEXR format) to create a film noir lighting setup. Target rotation is 125 degrees (2.18166 radians) for Z-axis Mapping node rotation to position the key light. Target saturation is 0.0 (monochromatic). Output files are saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/noir_setup.blend and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/noir_render.png. All path and value claims are correct except for rotation_tolerance_rad: the metadata claims 0.1 radians but the verifier actually uses 0.2 radians (double the claimed tolerance).
